key_insight: Estimating any valid transport map can be just as statistically hard as estimating the optimal transport map, under standard stability assumptions.
engineering_relevance: This gives a rigorous sample-complexity ceiling for transport-based generative methods such as diffusion models, flow matching, and normalizing flows when their learned object is used as a transport map or plan.
method: Formulate valid transport-map estimation in a minimax framework.
Derive lower bounds that apply to any method producing a transport map/plan.
Show that when OT stability assumptions hold, sub-optimal maps offer no statistical advantage; when they fail, they can be easier to learn.
evidence_signal: The paper is a theory note centered on minimax lower bounds and illustrative examples, rather than an empirical benchmark study.
watch_item: The strongest conclusions rely on OT stability assumptions, so practical gains from alternative transport maps may depend heavily on whether those assumptions hold in the target problem.
paper_title: The Fundamental Limits of Valid Transport Map Estimation
